line: GROVE_RESULT: focused F18 retrieval-side opt-in mood congruence landed; recall now preserves the `mood_congruent` flag and uses affect-aware ranking only when explicitly requested and affect data exists.

event.summary: focused F18 retrieval-side opt-in mood congruence landed; recall now preserves the `mood_congruent` flag and uses affect-aware ranking only when explicitly requested and affect data exists.

line: - The durable affect timeline and `mood_history` path were already present; the live gap was request-to-ranking wiring, not storage.

items: The durable affect timeline and `mood_history` path were already present; the live gap was request-to-ranking wiring, not storage.

line: - `mood_congruent` was being parsed and surfaced in degraded summaries, but dropped before normalized retrieval state and never consumed by ranking.

items: `mood_congruent` was being parsed and surfaced in degraded summaries, but dropped before normalized retrieval state and never consumed by ranking.

line: - The smallest safe implementation was to keep the behavior in the daemon retrieval path and reuse existing mood-history state, rather than broadening core storage/query contracts.

items: The smallest safe implementation was to keep the behavior in the daemon retrieval path and reuse existing mood-history state, rather than broadening core storage/query contracts.

line: - Kept mood congruence opt-in and inspectable by switching ranking profile only when `mood_congruent=true` and both current mood + candidate affect exist.

items: Kept mood congruence opt-in and inspectable by switching ranking profile only when `mood_congruent=true` and both current mood + candidate affect exist.

line: - Derived the “current mood snapshot” from the latest `mood_history` row instead of inventing a separate runtime state surface.

items: Derived the “current mood snapshot” from the latest `mood_history` row instead of inventing a separate runtime state surface.

line: - Left context-budget and graph-explain fallback behavior effectively balanced unless affect metadata is actually present at the candidate site.

items: Left context-budget and graph-explain fallback behavior effectively balanced unless affect metadata is actually present at the candidate site.

line: - The current congruence heuristic is intentionally small and bounded: it boosts salience/strength from valence/arousal similarity, but does not yet expose a dedicated explain reason per candidate.

items: The current congruence heuristic is intentionally small and bounded: it boosts salience/strength from valence/arousal similarity, but does not yet expose a dedicated explain reason per candidate.

line: - There are unrelated pre-existing warnings in `membrain-core/src/brain_store.rs` about unused compression imports; I did not touch them.

items: There are unrelated pre-existing warnings in `membrain-core/src/brain_store.rs` about unused compression imports; I did not touch them.

line: - Agent-mail broadcast intro was blocked by contact approval policy, so I coordinated locally and released reservations after validation.

items: Agent-mail broadcast intro was blocked by contact approval policy, so I coordinated locally and released reservations after validation.

line: - applied affect-aware ranking in the direct retrieval path
- added normalization test proving `mood_congruent` survives request normalization
- taught `fuse_scores(...)` to emit stable profile name `"mood_congruent"`
- added focused test for profile-name stability
